name: Deep-Fried Crab Balls with Vinegar Dip
recipeInstructions:
You can serve this dish hot or cold. We prefer the crab balls hot, when their exterior is crisp and the meat hot and moist. They also go better with the vinegar dip when hot. If you prefer to serve them cold or at room temperature, omit the dip, or substitute something like mustard and catsup.
Preparation: If using a food processor, the crab and pork fat should be cold. Cut pork fat into cubes. Trim and cut scallion into 1-inch sections, including greens. Using a steel blade, place water chestnuts and scallion in a bowl; pulse to chop vegetables. Add crab meat and pork fat; pulse to mince. Add eggs, sherry, salt, cornstarch, and minced ginger. Pulse to blend. Don't overprocess; you don't want a puree.
Deep-frying: In a wok or deep fryer, heat oil until bubbles form around a bamboo chopstick held upright in the oil. Using a teaspoon, form small balls of crab mixture. Test cooking temperature; it should brown and cook through in about 5 minutes. Adjust heat if necessary. Proceed with deep frying about 6 balls at a time; avoid crowding them. Drain on a bamboo strainer or towel. If not serving immediately, briefly reheat to crisp the skin. Avoid overcooking.
Vinegar dip: Mix ingredients in a shallow bowl; place on serving platter and surround with crab balls.
